question_text: Explain what is meant by the 'speed of sound' and state whether this speed is the same in all materials.
correct_answer: The speed of sound is the distance that a sound wave travels per unit time through a medium. The speed of sound is not the same in all materials; it is generally faster in solids and liquids than in gases.
explanation: Sound travels at different speeds depending on what material (medium) it passes through. In air, sound travels at about 340 m/s, but it moves much faster through liquids like water (about 1,500 m/s) and even faster through solids like steel (about 5,000 m/s). This is because the particles are packed more tightly together in solids and liquids, allowing sound waves to transfer energy between particles more quickly.
